revision.text:
[[ja:波留南方神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Minamikata Shrine (南方神社) | Religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Haru-minamikata shrine Akune Kagoshima.jpg | Location = 3096 Haru, Akune City, Kagoshima Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|32|00|35.1|N|130|12|24.0|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9150391}}
{{ill|Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神|1=Yasakatome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9150392}} | Type = Mukakusha (unranked shrine) | Established = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Haru Minamikata Shrine | Pilgrimage = | Festival = | Rituals = Kamimai (held every 8 years on the 28th day of the lunar July) | prefecture= {{ill|Akune|qid=Q15732|1=Akune, Kagoshima}} | coordinates= {{Coord|32.00975|N|130.206667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Haru Minamikata Shrine''' (波留南方神社, はるみなみかたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Haru, {{ill|Akune, Kagoshima|en|Akune, Kagoshima|qid=Q15732|lt=Akune, Kagoshima|1=Akune, Kagoshima}}, Japan. Its formal name is '''Minamikata Shrine''' (南方神社). It belongs to the lineage of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|qid=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} and was classified as an {{ill|unranked shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|qid=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/unranked shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.{{Cite web|title=Minamikata Shrine | 鹿児島県神社庁|url=https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/%E9%98%BF%E4%B9%85%E6%A0%B9%E5%B8%82/1230/|website=www.kagojinjacho.or.jp|accessdate=2022-01-04}} == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9150391}} * {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神|1=Yasakatome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9150392}} == History == It is said that the fifth lord of Akune, Mokune Hyōe-no-jō Naritomo, invited a Suwa Shrine from his former domain in Kumoda, Takae-go, {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Akune during the {{ill|Ryakuō|en|Ryakuō|qid=Q1146182|lt=Ryakuō era|1=Ryakuō}}. The shrine's sacred land extended to about 1 chō 5 tan. It is also recorded that Taira Kanetsugu of the Mokune-in lineage became the chief patron and dedicated a {{ill|Mirrors in Shinto|en|Mirrors in Shinto|qid=Q11591009|lt=Shinkyo|1=Mirrors in Shinto}} inscribed with the date of the 17th day of the fourth month of {{ill|Chōroku 2|ja|長禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed from Suwa Shrine to Minamikata Shrine. == Rituals == ;Kamimai (神舞, "Sacred Dance") :During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, this dance was dedicated every eight years in prayer for a bountiful harvest when the village headman changed. However, its origin remains unclear.{{Cite web|title=Haru Minamikata Shrine's Kamimai|url=http://www.pref.kagoshima.jp/ab10/kyoiku-bunka/bunka/museum/shichoson/akune/haru.html|website=Kagoshima Prefecture|accessdate=2022-01-04|language=ja}}{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Designated Cultural Property 3. Haru Minamikata Shrine's Kamimai|url=https://www.city.akune.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shogaigakushuuka/bunkagakari/4/2_1/925.html|website=www.city.akune.lg.jp|accessdate=2022-01-04|language=ja|publisher=Akune City}} :It is still performed every eight years on the 28th day of the lunar July. The presence of demon masks dating back to the {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|qid=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki era|1=Hōreki}} suggests the dance was performed at least since that period. On March 29, 1968, it was designated as a {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|lt=Folk Cultural Property|lt=Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財}} of Kagoshima. :The dance is primarily performed by young men from the Haru community, while musicians are typically older men. It consists of an introductory purification dance by children, followed by dances including Kami-oroshi (god descent), Bottle Dance, Bow Dance, Sword Dance, Tanokami Dance, General’s Dance, and Oni (demon) Dance. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|波留南方神社|version=103141136|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:波留南方神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11555589}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akune, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Akune]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
